document.docAuthor: Vu, Ha T., Eguchi, Katsuyuki, Le, Son X., Nguyen, Thu-Anh T. & Nguyen, Anh D.
document.docTitle: Otostigmus consonensis Vu & Eguchi & Le & Nguyen & Nguyen 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: A new species and a new record of the genus Otostigmus Porat, 1876 (Chilopoda Scolopendromorpha: Scolopendridae) in Vietnam

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A new species and a new record of the genus Otostigmus Porat, 1876 (Chilopoda Scolopendromorpha: Scolopendridae) in Vietnam

mods:name.mods:affiliation: VNU University of Science, Vietnam National University, 334 Nguyen Trai, Thanh Xuan, Hanoi, VIETNAM.
Graduate University of Science and Technology, Vietnam Academy of Science and Technology, 18, Hoangquocviet Rd., Caugiay District, Hanoi, VIETNAM.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Paratypes
. Six specimens; the same data as for holotype ; IEBR-Chi325 , IEBR-Chi331 , IEBR-Chi332 , IEBR- Chi333 , IEBR-Chi377 , IEBR-Chi379 .

Otostigmus consonensis
sp. nov.
differs from its congeners by 18 antennomeres (of them, 2.3–2.5 basal ones are glabrous); forcipular tooth plates with 3 + 3 teeth; tergites 5–20 with complete paramedian sutures; sternites with incomplete paramedian sutures; the ultimate leg with 11–15 prefemoral spines.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 1. Distribution localities of
Otostigmus consonensis
sp.nov. and
O. sulcipes Verhoeff, 1937
in Vietnam.
1: Me Linh; 2: Thach Nham; 3: Con Dao

paragraph.emphasis:
FIGURE 2.
Otostigmus consonensis
sp. nov. Holotype (IEBR-Chi326)

caption.paragraph: A: Cephalic plate and antennae, dorsal view; B: Two and half glabrous basal antennomeres, dorsal view; C: Ocelli, D: Coxosternal teeth.

subSubSection.paragraph: Color: Antennae, head and tergites blackish blue, whereas other parts yellow. Body length, ca . 30 mm ; width, LBS 10 сa . 1.2 mm .

Tergites 1–4 without sutures or keels; tergites 5–20 with complete paramedian sutures ( Fig. 3A presents the paramedian structure on tergites 9–10). The last tergite without sulcus ( Fig. 3C ). Spiracles oval, on LBS 3, 5, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20 ( Fig. 4A presents the spiracle on segment 10).

paragraph.emphasis:
FIGURE 3.
Otostigmus consonensis
sp. nov. Holotype (IEBR-Chi326)

caption.paragraph: A: Paramedian sutures on tergites 9–10; B: Sternites 9–10; C: The last tergite, dorsal view; D: Coxopleuron.

subSubSection.paragraph: The ultimate leg very long and slender, with 14 relatively thin prefemoral spines: 1 located on the corner, 2 dorsomedial, 7 medial and 4 ventromedial ones ( Fig. 4 C-D).

The number of prefemoral spines varies from 11 to 15, including 1–2 dorsomedial spines, 4–7 medial spines, and 2–5 ventromedial spines. The number of coxopleural spines also varies from 2 (1 apical, 1 subapical) to 4 (2 apical, 1 subapical, and 1 lateral).
Named after the “Con Son” Island , the largest island of Con Dao National Park, where type series were collected.
The new species was found only in the Con Son Island, which is about 80 km from the mainland of southern Vietnam .

paragraph.emphasis:
FIGURE 4.
Otostigmus consonensis
sp. nov. Holotype (IEBR-Chi326)

caption.paragraph: A: Spiracle on segment 10; B: Leg 1 with setae and tarsal spur; C: Right ultimate leg, dorsal view; D: prefemoral spines of right ultimate leg, ventral view.

subSubSection.paragraph:
The number of glabrous basal antennomeres is systematically important ( Schileyko 1995 ). By having 2–2.5 ones, the new species is close to
O. astenus
,
O. multidens
,
O. scaber
, and
O. spinosus
. The new species is different from
O. scaber
in the absence of longitudinal tergal keels and sternites lacking (or poorly developed) paramedian sutures.
Otostigmus consonensis
readily differs from all the aforementioned species (except for
O. astenus
) in having 3 + 3 (vs. 4 + 4 or even more) teeth of forcipular tooth plates. Two other diagnostic characters are the number of antennomeres (18 vs. 17 or 19–24) and the number of coxopleural spines (3–4 vs. more than 4), but both of them may be a subject in individual variability in
Otostigmus
s. str. and should not be used alone for distinguishing species in this taxon (as suggested by
Schileyko et al. , 2020
)

The new species is closely related to
O. voprosus
. Both have 18 antennomeres, a tooth plate with three distinct teeth, and tergites with paramedian sutures, but they can be distinguished by the number of glabrous basal antennomeres ( 2.3–2.5 in the new species vs. 2.5–3 in
O. voprosus
), the number of coxopleural spines (3–4 vs. 5), and the number of tarsal spurs on legs 2 and 3 (2 vs. 1). The phylogenetic analysis also revealed that
O. voprosus
is a sister species of the new one ( Fig. 9 ).

A detailed comparison between the new species and
Otostigmus species
recorded in Vietnam is presented in Table 2 .

caption.paragraph:
TABLE 2. Character comparison between
O. consonensis
sp. nov. and other Vietnamese
Otostigmus species

subSection.paragraph:
(Data from Schileyko (1992 , 1995 ), Lewis (2001) and
Vu et al. (2020))
